title: Partnering with technology: the importance of human machine teaming in future MDC2 systems
one_line_takeaway: Operator-centered human-machine teaming should shape MDC2/COP design through cognitive task analysis, user stories, iterative mockups, and decision-support layers.
d4d_relevance: Useful for designing the human-facing layer of a T3 semantic maritime COP: it supports building AI-assisted displays that reflect operator workflows, expose layered operational context, and help decision-makers trust and act on machine-generated recommendations even when the network is degraded.
input_data: derived user stories and future concept ideas
methods_or_architecture: combined HMT systems engineering with MDC2 COP research
used modified cognitive task analysis to elicit operator needs
translated findings into user stories and concept designs
built annotated mockups for iterative operator review
fed design seeds into a prototype exploration environment
outputs_metrics: user stories capturing operator decision-support needs
future concept ideas for MDC2 workflows
no quantitative performance metrics reported in the provided text
prototype_hooks: run short CTA-style interviews with maritime watchstanders to derive COP user stories
design layered COP views for tracks, alerts, uncertainty, comms state, mission intent, and decision options
add AI recommendation panels that explain evidence, confidence, and operator override paths
use annotated mockups as rapid hackathon artifacts before coding full features
map semantic T3 objects to operator tasks rather than only showing raw tracks
include feedback loops so operators can correct AI assumptions during degraded connectivity
pitch_evidence: paper comes from MITRE-linked MDC2 exploration work
requirements were grounded in C2 operator interviews rather than purely technical assumptions
supports the argument that AI in COP systems must be designed as teammate-facing decision support
iterative mockups provide a credible low-cost path for validating a hackathon prototype
reinforces the need for layered COP content and explainable support capabilities
limitations_risks: does not specifically address maritime operations or denied networks
appears focused on concepts and mockups rather than deployed system validation
operator sample size and interview details are unknown
no latency, resilience, accuracy, or mission-effectiveness metrics are provided
human-machine teaming guidance does not by itself solve disconnected data synchronization
